name: Modi
description: Modi, whose name translates to 'Courage' or 'Wrathful,' is a Norse god associated with courage, battle, and berserker rage. He is the son of Thor, the god of thunder, and the giantess Járnsaxa. Modi is destined to survive Ragnarok, the end of the world in Norse mythology, along with his brother Magni. After Ragnarok, Modi and Magni are prophesied to inherit their father's hammer, Mjolnir, signifying their strength and responsibility in rebuilding the world. While not as prominently featured as some other Norse deities, Modi embodies the qualities of bravery and fierce determination. His role in the post-Ragnarok world highlights the continuation of strength and valor within the new order. Accounts of Modi are relatively scarce, but his lineage and prophesied role underscore his importance as a symbol of resilience and the enduring power of the Aesir gods. His potential is seen in his future wielding of Mjolnir, implying a great capacity for wielding divine power. Modi exemplifies a continuation of the warrior spirit and a future of courage in the face of adversity.
subtitle: God of Courage and Post-Ragnarok Strength

keyMyths.title: The Lineage of Modi
keyMyths.description: Modi is the son of Thor and the giantess Járnsaxa, inheriting strength from both sides of his family. This lineage marks him as a powerful warrior.

keyMyths.title: Survival of Ragnarok
keyMyths.description: Modi is prophesied to survive Ragnarok along with his brother Magni, highlighting his importance for the world's future. They will inherit Mjolnir.

keyMyths.title: Inheriting Mjolnir
keyMyths.description: After Ragnarok, Modi and Magni inherit Mjolnir from their father Thor, symbolizing their strength and responsibility. This signifies a new era.

sources.author: Snorri Sturluson

sources.text: A collection of Old Norse poems that provide insights into Norse mythology and cosmology.

cultural.modernLegacy: Modi, though not as prominent as his father Thor, sees modern recognition through comic books, video games, and other media. He represents themes of courage and the continuation of strength through generations, often depicted as a fierce warrior or a symbol of resilience.

extendedContent.title: Origins & Birth
extendedContent.content: Modi's lineage marks him as a figure of immense power, born from the union of Thor, the thunder god and protector of Midgard, and Járnsaxa, a giantess. This parentage places him within both the Aesir and Jotnar, inheriting traits from both sides. While the specific details of Modi's birth are scarce in surviving Norse texts, the implications of his parentage are significant. As a son of Thor, he is destined for greatness and inherits a portion of his father's strength. His mother, Járnsaxa, whose name translates to 'iron knife' or 'iron scone,' suggests a connection to strength and resilience, contributing to Modi's association with courage and wrath. This dual heritage foreshadows his role in the events following Ragnarok, where he is prophesied to help rebuild the world.

extendedContent.title: Major Myths & Stories
extendedContent.content: Unlike some of the more prominent Norse gods, Modi does not feature in many surviving myths. However, his significance lies in his prophesied role in the events of Ragnarok and its aftermath. The primary mention of Modi is in connection with his brother Magni, in that both are fated to survive Ragnarok, the twilight of the gods, when the world is consumed by fire and chaos. After the cataclysm, Modi and Magni are destined to inherit Mjolnir, Thor's mighty hammer, symbolizing their inheritance of Thor's strength and responsibility. This suggests that Modi will play a crucial role in the new world order, helping to rebuild and maintain balance. Though the exact nature of their actions is not detailed, the inheritance of Mjolnir signifies a continuation of the protection and order that Thor represented.

extendedContent.title: Legacy & Influence
extendedContent.content: While Modi is not as widely known as other Norse deities like Odin, Thor, or Loki, his legacy persists through his role in the post-Ragnarok world. The prophecy of Modi and Magni inheriting Mjolnir emphasizes the cyclical nature of Norse cosmology – destruction followed by rebirth. This provides a sense of hope and continuity within the often-bleak outlook of Norse mythology. In modern times, Modi appears as a character in various forms of media, including comic books, video games, and novels, often depicted as a strong and courageous warrior, reflecting his association with courage and battle prowess. These portrayals, while often fictionalized, help to keep the memory of Modi alive and introduce him to new audiences, ensuring his place in the ongoing fascination with Norse mythology.

shortDescription: Modi is the Norse god of courage and battle, son of Thor and the giantess Járnsaxa. Destined to survive Ragnarok, he will inherit Mjolnir with his brother.
